evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/kyber.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site describes real-time infrastructure for machine control across robots, drones, vehicles, remote desktops, and immersive applications.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The product page says Kyber synchronizes video, audio, control inputs, and telemetry to one clock over QUIC and HTTP/3 with end-to-end encryption.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The about page says the team is building infrastructure for robots, drones, and autonomous systems, and lists open roles for systems and deployed engineers.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is whether low-latency control claims can hold across unpredictable field networks and safety-critical machine environments.
TODO: State the most important weekly evidence gap or product risk without guessing.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/frontier-health.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site describes Juno as a supportive AI teammate for healthcare administration teams, automating repetitive high-volume work across existing systems.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The public workflow examples cover outpatient referrals, waiting-list management, appointment booking and rescheduling, patient communications, and patient tracking.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: TNW says Frontier Health deployed in an NHS environment in eight weeks and reported 221 staff days saved plus a 22 percent median emergency-department time reduction.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is whether healthcare buyers can validate safety, escalation, and audit controls fast enough for broader workflow automation.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/gradial.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official homepage says Gradial creates execution velocity from brief to live across the enterprise content supply chain.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The site names agent jobs for content and campaign execution, asset execution, generative engine optimization, and insights and journeys.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: Gradial shows enterprise customer stories and logos including T-Mobile, AWS, Prudential, Cisco, Microsoft, Electronic Arts, and Weber.

risks: Research risk candidate: The risk is that brand governance and approval complexity may limit how much campaign execution agents can own without human review.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/verse.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The homepage says Verse gets data centers online up to three years faster and uses Aria to read market prices, weather, and live telemetry.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The Series B announcement says Dispatch Intelligence orchestrates on-site energy resources alongside existing grid infrastructure.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The founders letter says Aria grew from contract management, PPA tools, risk management, and utility bill management for large energy users.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is whether software-driven dispatch can overcome slow utility approvals, grid constraints, and site-specific energy economics.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/turbo-law.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site describes a Fact Graph that links facts, events, records, people, and issues across time and causation for litigation teams.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The product surface describes a Unified Matter Ecosystem for documents, facts, timelines, emails, drafts, research, reports, strategy, templates, and client updates.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: Tech.eu says Turbo Law supports review, drafting, research, exposure analysis, strategy, and assistant workflows for defense law firms and insurers.

risks: Research risk candidate: The risk is that firms may still require strict human review if privilege boundaries or ethical-wall enforcement vary by matter and client.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/devplan.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The homepage describes Daily Digest, Automated Status, Ask Devplan, Change History, and Weaver as product intelligence workflows.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The site says Weaver converts commits, tickets, meetings, Slack, Notion, Google Workspace, and customer feedback into a shared product graph.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The funding announcement says Weaver connects GitHub, Jira, Linear, Slack, Notion, Google Workspace, meeting notes, and customer feedback.

risks: Research risk candidate: The risk is that organizational politics and incomplete tool coverage limit how much teams accept one generated graph as the source of truth.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/cargofy.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The homepage says Cargofy provides a digital workforce for logistics that handles jobs from quote to delivery through calls, email, and WhatsApp.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The official site says its AI workers quote, dispatch, track, and close loads for forwarders, brokers, carriers, 3PLs, and shippers.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The careers page says Cargofy builds an AI-native operating system for global freight with autonomous agents for procurement, dispatching, and operations.

risks: Research risk candidate: The risk is that freight exceptions, compliance steps, and carrier relationships may still require human operators despite automation claims.
